# tests/fixtures/replay-corpus/

Frozen JSONL corpora of historical-shape decisions that the current engine
must reproduce byte-identically. Used by `scripts/replay-decisions.js` and the
`scripts/check-replay-corpus.sh` CI gate (HAP ADR-007 PR-D).

Each line is a single decision case:

```json
{
  "tag":   "F3:rm-rf-tmp",
  "input": { "tool": "Bash", "command": "rm -rf /tmp/build", "branch": "feature/test" },
  "expected": {
    "action":         "require-tests",
    "decisionSource": "risk-engine",
    "floorFired":     null,
    "irHash":         "sha256:..."
  }
}
```

The replay gate runs each `input` through `runtime/decision-engine.decide()`
and `runtime/action-ir.build()` under an isolated state directory and asserts
the four `expected.*` fields stay byte-identical. Drift on any case fails CI.

## Files

- `corpus.jsonl` — ~50 synthetic no-PII decisions covering every reachable
  in-process lattice rung (F3, F4, F6, F7, F8, F9) plus every baseline
  risk-engine outcome (allow / route / modify / require-tests / require-review /
  escalate / block). Generated by `build-corpus.js` in this directory.

- `adversarial.jsonl` — focused IR/replay-stability adversarial seed: cosmetic
  whitespace variants, NFKD + confusables-folded variants, ADR-008 §4.2 alias
  nests, numeric/null/array command shapes, and boundary IR fields. Generated
  by `build-adversarial.js` in this directory.

The two generators live alongside the JSONL fixtures (not under `scripts/`)
because their CASES tables embed synthetic risky literals — `rm -rf`,
`curl | bash`, `npx -y`, `--no-preserve-root` — that `scripts/audit-local.sh`
intentionally rejects in top-level `scripts/`. The audit gate is scoped to
`scripts/`, `claude/hooks/`, and `.github/workflows/`, and does not scan
fixtures.

`scripts/bench-ir.js` also reads these JSONL files for its benchmark input
set, so the perf and replay gates share one source of truth.

## Updating the corpora

When the engine makes an intentional behavior change that breaks replay:

1. Inspect the drift report from `bash scripts/check-replay-corpus.sh`.
2. Confirm the change is intentional (ADR / DECISIONS.md entry).
3. Regenerate the affected corpus:
   ```bash
   node tests/fixtures/replay-corpus/build-corpus.js
   node tests/fixtures/replay-corpus/build-adversarial.js
   ```
4. Re-run the gate; commit the refreshed fixture alongside the engine change.

The generators isolate each call (fresh `HORUS_STATE_DIR`, `resetCache()`,
contract disabled) so generated outputs do not depend on the order of cases.

## Rungs NOT covered here

Floors that require contract / envelope / session state to fire are exercised
in their own fixtures, not in the static corpus:

| Rung | Floor | Lives in |
|---|---|---|
| F1 | kill-switch | env-toggled at runtime |
| F2 | contract-hash-mismatch | `tests/fixtures/contract/` |
| F5 | strict-gated-no-cover | `tests/fixtures/contract/` |
| F11 | validity-window | `tests/fixtures/contract/` |
| F12 | mcp-deny | `tests/fixtures/contract/` |
| F13 | skill-deny | `tests/fixtures/contract/` |
| F14 | budget-exceeded | `tests/fixtures/contract/` |
| F14b | session-over-duration | `tests/fixtures/contract/` |
| F10 | taint-floor | `tests/fixtures/decision-engine/` |
| F18 | network-egress | `tests/fixtures/network-egress/` |
| F15 | execution-envelope | `tests/fixtures/decision-engine/` |
